perturbation.original_text: 3. FRANCHISE FEE. (a) Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which shall be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate is $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a percentage share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined on a pro rata basis according to its percentage share of the total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales occurring within the localities during the calendar year. For each calendar year of the Franchise, each locality's percentage share shall be determined by the following formula: total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales within Locality's percentage share = the Territorial Limits of the locality total dollar value of Grantee's gas sales in the three localities For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee shall be paid to Grantor on or before March 31, 2017.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. FRANCHISE FEE. (a) Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which may be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate may be $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a discretionary share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined based on Grantee's sole discretion. For each calendar year of the Franchise, each locality's percentage share shall be determined at the sole discretion of the Grantee. For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee may be paid to Grantor at Grantee's convenience.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Franchise Fee "shall be calculated pursuant to this Section," providing a formula for determination. The modified text changes this to "may be calculated pursuant to this Section," making the calculation method optional. Similarly, the original text stipulates that the total annual Franchise Fee "is $98,196," while the modified text suggests it "may be $98,196". Changing 'shall be' to 'discretionary' and 'may be' introduces ambiguity and contradicts the following sentence about the formula, it is creating uncertainty about the enforcement of the franchisee Fee. The phrase "Grantee's sole discretion" directly contradicts the original terms of the agreement regarding the Franchise Fee calculation, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. FRANCHISE FEE. (a) Grantee shall pay to Grantor a Franchise Fee which may be calculated pursuant to this Section. It is understood that Grantee has or will enter into franchise agreements with the City of Roanoke ("Roanoke") and the City of Salem ("Salem") and the Town of Vinton ("Vinton") (Grantor, Roanoke and Salem being hereinafter sometimes collectively referred to as the "localities" and singularly as a "locality") with fee provisions identical to this one, and that the total annual Franchise Fee to be paid to the three localities in aggregate may be $98,196 for calendar year 2016 ("base year total annual Franchise Fee"). Grantor's Franchise Fee shall be a discretionary share of the base year total annual Franchise Fee, which shall be determined based on Grantee's sole discretion. For each calendar year of the Franchise, each locality's percentage share shall be determined at the sole discretion of the Grantee. For calendar year 2016, the Franchise Fee may be paid to Grantor at Grantee's convenience.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces significant ambiguity and discretion where the original text provided specific obligations and calculations. The original text states the Franchise Fee 'shall be calculated pursuant to this Section,' and the changed text alters this to 'may be calculated pursuant to this Section.' This change, along with 'discretionary share' and 'Grantee's sole discretion,' directly contradicts the initial requirement for a defined calculation method, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 3 itself. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same and the Change itself causes this direct contradiction, this is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. BUSINESS OFFICE. Grantee shall during the term of this Franchise maintain at least one business office within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office shall be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. BUSINESS OFFICE. Grantee may during the term of this Franchise maintain at least one business office within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office may be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Grantee "shall... maintain at least one business office," creating a clear obligation. By changing "shall" to "may", the obligation is removed, making it optional for the Grantee to maintain a business office. Similarly, the original text requires the office to "be open at least forty (40) hours per week," establishing a minimum operating hour requirement. This change introduces ambiguity, and therefore in-text contradiction, where earlier the Grantee was obligated to do something, with this perturbation, now it is at their discretion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. BUSINESS OFFICE. Grantee may during the term of this Franchise maintain at least one business office within the Territorial Limits of Grantor. Such office may be open at least forty (40) hours per week for the conduct of business between Grantee and its customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that the Grantee "shall... maintain at least one business office," and that such office "be open at least forty (40) hours per week." The changed text modifies "shall" to "may" in both instances. This change creates a direct contradiction because it transforms a mandatory obligation into an optional one, making it unclear whether the Grantee is actually required to have an office or maintain specific operating hours. This aligns with Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, because the clause is effectively placed in multiple locations (the original version and the changed version) with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 5. NONDISCRIMINATION. Grantee shall not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other basis prohibited by state law relating to discrimination in employment, except where there is a bona fide occupational qualification reasonably necessary to the normal operation of the Grantee.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. NONDISCRIMINATION. Grantee shall endeavor not to discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other basis prohibited by state law relating to discrimination in employment, except where there is a bona fide occupational qualification reasonably necessary to the normal operation of the Grantee.
perturbation.explanation: By inserting 'endeavor' the obligation on Nondiscrimination has been reduced to an effort. While the clause still contains 'shall not discriminate', this now means 'shall try not to discriminate' creating an in-text contradiction within the section. The 'endeavor' makes enforcement ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. NONDISCRIMINATION. Grantee shall endeavor not to discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other basis prohibited by state law relating to discrimination in employment, except where there is a bona fide occupational qualification reasonably necessary to the normal operation of the Grantee.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Grantee 'shall not discriminate'. The changed text modifies this to 'shall endeavor not to discriminate'. The insertion of 'endeavor' weakens the obligation, creating an internal contradiction within the section itself. This is because 'shall not' indicates a strict prohibition, while 'shall endeavor not to' implies an attempt to comply but not a guarantee, making the requirement ambiguous and difficult to enforce. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are the same, and the explanation highlights the contradiction, it is clear a contradiction exists.
